_meta.keyKnowledge: We are learning how authors use vivid vocabulary and figurative language — like metaphors, similes, personification and idioms — to create meaning and effect.

_source.w: We are learning how authors use vivid vocabulary and figurative language — like metaphors, similes, personification and idioms — to create meaning and effect.
y: Figurative language is what transforms ordinary writing into writing that resonates with readers.
Understanding how these techniques work helps us appreciate literature and use them in our own writing.
Knowing about idioms and expressions helps us navigate the language we encounter in everyday life.
eg: We analyse how an author uses a metaphor to make an abstract concept feel concrete and real.
We identify idioms in everyday language — like 'it's raining cats and dogs' — and discuss their meaning.
We examine how personification brings non-human things to life in a poem.
We compare the effect of a literal description with a figurative one.
We experiment with using vivid vocabulary and figurative language in our own writing.
We create a class collection of effective figurative language examples from our reading.
